data.title: SHIB faces crucial $0.0000138 test; breakout may lead to $0.000020-$0.000024 by October 2025.
data.description: Shiba Inu is currently at a critical point, as it approaches the 200-day EMA test at $0.0000138. If there is a bullish breakout, there is a possibility for SHIB to reach between $0.000020 and $0.000024 in the next 4-6 weeks. However, failure to break out could lead to a decline to $0.0000121.

data.title: Stock market rally at highs, Tesla racing, Nvidia, Meta eye buy points as Fed meeting looms.
data.description: The stock market is currently experiencing a significant rally, with Tesla leading the charge while Nvidia and Meta are preparing for potential growth. The upcoming Federal Reserve meeting is generating a sense of anticipation among investors. Dow Jones Futures reflect this market surge, with attention drawn to Nvidia and Meta potentially reaching buy points and Tesla continuing its rapid pace.

data.title: AION V model by GAC earns 5-star safety rating in Euro NCAP tests for 2025, indicating top safety.
data.description: In Leuven, Belgium, on September 12, 2025, it was reported that the AION V, a vehicle manufactured by GAC, obtained an impressive five-star safety rating in the rigorous 2025 European New Car Assessment Programme (Euro NCAP) evaluations. This recognition highlights the AION V's commitment to safety standards.

data.title: StarCharge V2G products showcased at IAA Mobility 2025 in Munich, a global event, highlighting EV charging solutions.
data.description: In Munich on September 12, 2025, StarCharge, a top company in EV charging infrastructure and smart energy solutions, showcased at IAA Mobility 2025, a key global event for mobility, sustainability, and tech. The company made a significant impression alongside a prominent automotive partner.

data.title: Trump urges G7, EU to impose tariffs on China and India for buying Russian oil, aiming to end Ukraine conflict. Treasury seeks coordinated action.
data.description: The U.S. government is calling on its G7 and EU allies to impose tariffs on China and India for buying Russian oil amidst a historic increase in U.S. tariff revenues under President Donald Trump's trade policies. Finance ministers from the G7 nations recently gathered for a call to discuss strategies for putting more pressure on Moscow to end the conflict in Ukraine. During the meeting chaired by Canadian Finance Minister Francois-Philippe Champagne, it was agreed to expedite discussions on using frozen Russian assets to aid Ukraine's defense efforts. U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent urged other countries to enact tariffs on those supporting Russia's war through energy transactions. Bessent, along with U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, emphasized the necessity of coordinated actions in a joint statement. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
